name: SPL AST Evaluator
description: Tree-walk evaluator for SPL (Security Policy Language) ASTs with gas and depth metering. Implements 20 built-in operations including logical operators, comparisons, set operations, time operations, accessors, counters, and cryptographic predicates. Enforces resource limits via gas budget (default 10,000) and maximum recursion depth (64).

types.name: GasExhausted
types.description: Exception raised when gas budget is exhausted during evaluation

types.name: DepthExceeded
types.description: Exception raised when maximum nesting depth is exceeded during evaluation

types.name: SPLError
types.description: Exception raised for SPL evaluation errors (unknown operations, unresolved symbols in strict mode)

types.name: _EvalState
types.description: Internal state for tracking gas consumption and recursion depth during evaluation

functions.name: __init__
functions.description: Initialize evaluation state with a maximum gas budget and zero depth

functions.name: eval_policy
functions.description: Evaluate an SPL AST within a context dictionary. Entry point for policy evaluation with automatic gas/depth metering setup.

inputs.name: ast
inputs.description: SPL abstract syntax tree (typically list or symbol)

inputs.name: ctx
inputs.description: Context dictionary with keys: req, vars, now, per_day_count, crypto, maxGas/max_gas

error_cases.name: gas_exhausted
error_cases.condition: Gas budget is exhausted during evaluation

error_cases.name: unresolved_symbol
error_cases.condition: Symbol cannot be resolved and ctx['strict'] is True

side_effects: Calls functions from ctx['per_day_count'] and ctx['crypto'] if present

structured_side_effects.description: Creates and modifies internal evaluation state

functions.name: _eval
functions.description: Internal recursive evaluator with gas and depth metering. Decrements gas, increments depth, delegates to _eval_inner, and restores depth.

inputs.name: st
inputs.description: Evaluation state tracking gas and depth

postconditions: st.depth is restored to original value after evaluation

functions.name: _eval_inner
functions.description: Core evaluation logic handling all 20 SPL built-in operations: logical (and, or, not), comparison (=, <=, <, >=, >), sets (member, in, subset?), time (before), accessors (get, tuple), counter (per-day-count), and crypto predicates (dpop_ok?, merkle_ok?, vrf_ok?, thresh_ok?)

error_cases.name: unknown_operation
error_cases.condition: node[0] does not match any known operation

error_cases.name: index_error
error_cases.condition: Insufficient arguments for operation (e.g., 'not' with no args)

postconditions: Returns evaluated result based on operation type
side_effects: May call ctx['crypto'] callbacks (dpop_ok, merkle_ok, vrf_ok, thresh_ok)

structured_side_effects.description: Calls external counter function if provided
Calls crypto verification functions if provided

functions.name: _resolve_symbol
functions.description: Resolve a symbol to its value from context. Handles special symbols (#t, #f, req, now) and variable lookups from ctx['vars']. In strict mode, raises SPLError for unresolved symbols.

inputs.name: ctx
inputs.description: Context dictionary containing vars, req, and strict flag

error_cases.name: unresolved_symbol
error_cases.condition: x is a string symbol not in vars and ctx['strict'] is True

postconditions: Returns resolved value or original input if not a symbol

functions.name: _truthy
functions.description: Convert a value to boolean using SPL truthiness rules: None is false, booleans as-is, numbers are false if zero, all other values are true.

functions.name: _to_float
functions.description: Convert a value to float. Returns 0.0 if conversion fails (TypeError or ValueError).
postconditions: Returns float representation, or 0.0 on conversion failure

invariants: Gas is decremented by 1 for each _eval call
Depth is incremented before evaluation and decremented after
Evaluation stops when gas < 0 or depth > MAX_DEPTH

data_access.rationale: Reads from context dict (ctx) to access variables, request data, and callback functions. Does not persist state between calls.

side_effects.rationale: Invokes callback functions provided in ctx['per_day_count'] and ctx['crypto'] for external operations

authority.rationale: This component is the authoritative evaluator for SPL AST execution with resource metering
